title: Greta Gerwig on ‘Lady Bird,’ One of the Year’s Best Movies | The Big Picture (Ep. 34)
format: Greta Gerwig talks about her solo writing-directing debut, why she set it in 2002 Sacramento, casting from the theater, and reviving Dave Matthews.

films.title: Lady Bird
films.note: Her debut as sole writer-director; a Sacramento coming-of-age story she began drafting in 2013 and financed off a finished script, with Scott Rudin and Eli Bush coming aboard without asking for changes.

films.title: Frances Ha
films.note: Co-written with Noah Baumbach; she cites it as proof she can build true-feeling detail from a life she hasn't lived.

films.title: Mistress America
films.note: Another Baumbach co-write; long scripting time and no on-set improvisation.

films.title: Manchester by the Sea
films.note: Where she'd seen Lucas Hedges before handing him the script.

films.title: The Shape of Water
films.note: Her pick for the last great film she'd seen, watched the day before; a showcase for Sally Hawkins.

films.title: Vera Drake
films.note: The Mike Leigh film where she first noticed Sally Hawkins.

films.title: Happy-Go-Lucky
films.note: Another Leigh/Hawkins performance she brings up as evidence Hawkins is great every time.
